title: podman seems to ignore certs.d directories when running rootless.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->
/kind bug

**Description**

When running podman pull/push rootless, I'm forced to pass the --cert-dir option to provide the certificates (tried the defaults  /etc/containers/certs.d/<certs>, or /etc/containers/<host>/<certs>, or even /etc/docker/certs.d/<host>/<certs>.

I'm ok, passing the --cert-dir option, for pull/push, but, for podman image sign, I don't have the option, and I get the complaint from the 
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. setup a secure docker registry with tls (using my certs)
2. as an unprivileged user, build an image
3. tag the image and push it to the registry
* podman push --cert-dir <path-to-my-certs>
4. try to sign the image with your gpg key
* podman image sign --sign-by <my-gpg-key> docker://<registry:image:tag>
**Describe the results you received:**

podman produces error:

error getting manifest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://<registry:<port>/v2/: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority

**Describe the results you expected:**

Either, podman image sing allows passing the --cert-dir option, or the /etc/containers/certs.d/ directory is honored.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:         1.0.0
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:        linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

host:

    version: 'conmon version 1.14.0-dev, commit: 635328ab9b2058e40335533f35d595604666f1e4-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "7.6"
   MemFree: 686776320
   MemTotal: 8201482240
    OCIRuntime:
      package:  runc-1.0.0-59.dev.git2abd837.el7.x86_64
      path: /usr/bin/runc
      version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
    SwapFree: 930344960
    SwapTotal: 1073737728
    arch: amd64
    cpus: 4
    hostname: my-server.test.com
    kernel: 3.10.0-957.5.1.el7.x86_64
    os: linux
    rootless: true
    uptime: 478h 38m 58.07s (Approximately 19.92 days)
insecure registries:
  registries:
    - my-insecure-registry.test.com:5000
registries:

    - registry.access.redhat.com
    - docker.io

  ConfigFile: /home/my_user/.config/containers/storage.conf

    number: 75

    number: 10

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Vmware guest

comments.body:
Hey, any comments? I have exactly same problem - I have CA certs used to sign registry's cert in my system database, and additionally in `/etc/containers/certs.d/<hostname>/*.crt`. With either `strace` or `podman --log-level debug` I see podman is reading those, but login fails unless I pass `--tls-verify=false`. The error is

```
Error: error authenticating creds for "registry.myhost.local": pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry.myhost.local/v2/: EOF
```

Any hints how to debug this further?

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.7
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-4.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.0.0-dev, commit: 164df8af4e62dc759c312eab4b97ea9fb6b5f1fc'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
```
@zdzichu, thanks for the ping. We're looking into it now.
There's no specific reason. I just didn't take `--cert-dir` into account. Do I need to add `--cert-dir`?

> There's no specific reason. I just didn't take `--cert-dir` into account. Do I need to add `--cert-dir`?

Yes, I think we to support `--cert-dir`.

Needing to pass the --cert-dir when in rootless mode and the certs are in the default location seems like a bug that needs to be chased down.  I'm fine with adding --cert-dir to the `podman image sign` command, but it to shouldn't be necessary for the default case.

> > 
> 
> @giuseppe, have you seen this before?

AFAIK, there is no difference between rootless and root for the cert.d location, could you confirm that it works when running as root?  Could you attach the output with `--log-level debug`?
The above error `podman produces error: error getting manifest:` was returned by `GetManifest()`. I don't think the sign --cert-dir can avoid this error.
@QiWang19 I don't understand, are you saying this can not be done?

> @QiWang19 I don't understand, are you saying this can not be done?

This can be done. Created #3833 PR for --cert-dir
we believe this issue is now closed with PR #3833 .  please re-open if you find that not to be true.
